name: Check Into Cash
description_short: Check Into Cash Gladstone, Missouri — Check Into Cash is a national payday and title lender with 30+ years in business, offering short-term loans up to ...
description_long:
Check Into Cash is one of the oldest and largest payday lending chains in the United States, having operated for over 30 years. The company was founded in 1993 and has grown to hundreds of store locations across the country, including multiple Michigan locations. The Ferndale, MI branch at 749 East 9 Mile Road operates Monday through Saturday and serves customers needing short-term financial solutions. The company holds a Michigan state lending license and posts a retail fee schedule as required by state law.

The company's core product is the payday loan, available in Michigan up to $600, repayable on the borrower's next payday. Beyond payday loans, Check Into Cash offers installment loans with longer repayment terms, title loans secured against a vehicle's clean title, and a Flex Line of Credit that allows repeated borrowing up to an approved limit. The Ferndale location also provides non-lending financial services including check cashing, bill payment, Western Union money transfers, and Green Dot Visa Debit Cards. The main corporate site lists tax preparation and money orders as additional services at select locations.

What distinguishes Check Into Cash from smaller or newer operators is its three-decade track record and the breadth of services offered in a single storefront. Customers can apply for a loan in-store or start the process online, and approved borrowers can receive cash the same day they visit a branch. The application requirements are relatively straightforward: a government-issued photo ID, a Social Security number, proof of income, and an active checking account with routing and account numbers. The company emphasizes a quick and confidential process.

Honestly, Check Into Cash is a high-cost lender of last resort. Payday loans in Michigan carry fees that translate to triple-digit annual percentage rates, and the $600 cap limits how much help the product can provide. Borrowers who roll over or re-borrow repeatedly can accumulate significant fees. Title loans carry the additional risk of vehicle repossession. These products are best understood as tools for genuine emergencies—not as a regular financial strategy. Consumers who have access to credit unions, employer advances, or personal loans at lower rates should exhaust those options first.
pros: Payday loans available both in-store and online, giving flexibility in how you apply
30+ years in business with a Michigan state lending license posted publicly
Same-day cash disbursement for approved in-store applicants
Multiple financial services in one location: loans, check cashing, Western Union, bill pay, and Green Dot cards
Straightforward eligibility — approval based on income verification and active checking account, not credit score
Extended weekday hours (8am–7pm Friday) and Saturday availability for working borrowers
Offers multiple loan types — payday, installment, title, and flex line of credit — to match different borrowing needs
cons: Payday loans carry triple-digit APRs typical of the industry — cost is very high relative to the amount borrowed
Michigan payday loan cap is $600, which may not cover larger emergency expenses
Requires an active checking account — unbanked consumers cannot access payday loan products
Title loans risk vehicle repossession if the borrower cannot repay
Closed Sundays with limited Saturday hours (closes 4pm), reducing accessibility on weekends
best_for: Employed consumers with a checking account facing a small, unexpected expense before their next paycheck
People who need cash the same day and have already been declined by banks or credit unions
Borrowers who also need check cashing or bill payment and want to handle multiple financial tasks in one stop
Vehicle owners who need more than the $600 payday loan cap and have a clear title to borrow against
diagnosis: Check Into Cash is best for employed consumers with an active bank account who face a small, urgent cash shortfall and need same-day funds without a credit check. The main caveat is cost: payday and title loan fees translate to extremely high APRs, making these products suitable only for genuine short-term emergencies — not recurring cash flow gaps.
services: Same-day cash disbursement for approved applicants
